ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301638 covers mineral water as a category; no Evian-brand-specific term exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 11713004 is the closest parent concept for water as a substance; no mineral-water-specific branded code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml Evian bottle, as served at Din Tai Fung Singapore and other restaurants, equivalent to approximately 330 g of water.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, water, tap, drinking — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 14555); cross-referenced with Evian product label data (Danone) for mineral composition
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Plain water (mineral water) has 0 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. USDA FoodData Central entry for water confirms all core macros are zero. Evian product label (Danone) confirms sodium 6.5 mg/L, calcium 80 mg/L, magnesium 26 mg/L, potassium 1 mg/L, bicarbonate 360 mg/L. Values converted to per-100 g (per-100 ml) basis.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of water — University of Sydney GI Database; water contains no glycemic carbohydrates and is assigned a conventional GI of 0 (represented here as 1 per Veritas convention for numeric validity)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Water contains no digestible carbohydrates and therefore has no measurable glycemic response. The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature confirm water does not raise blood glucose. GI is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive integer per Veritas numeric convention) to satisfy schema requirements; glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is 0. gi_category is low by convention.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (0.65 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: Evian mineral water contains only 0.65 mg sodium per 100 ml, making it an excellent hydration choice for individuals managing hypertension who must restrict dietary sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates and zero glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Evian mineral water contains no carbohydrates or sugars and has a glycemic load of zero, making it the preferred beverage for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes seeking to maintain stable postprandial blood glucose levels.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, and beneficial mineral content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Evian mineral water is free of fat, cholesterol, and added sugars, and its naturally occurring calcium (8 mg/100 ml) and magnesium (2.6 mg/100 ml) may support vascular function; it is a heart-healthy hydration option for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Evian Natural Mineral Water is a premium still bottled water sourced from the French Alps, specifically from the Cachat spring in Évian-les-Bains, France. The water is naturally filtered through Alpine glacial rock over a 15-year journey, acquiring a balanced mineral profile including calcium (80 mg/L), magnesium (26 mg/L), sodium (6.5 mg/L), potassium (1 mg/L), and bicarbonate (360 mg/L). It contains zero calories, zero protein, zero fat, and zero carbohydrates, making it nutritionally inert from a macronutrient perspective. The 330 ml single-serve bottle is commonly offered as a beverage option at restaurants in Singapore, including Din Tai Fung. Evian is classified as a minimally processed natural mineral water and is suitable for all dietary patterns including vegetarian, vegan, halal, kosher, and gluten-free diets. Clinically, it is ideal for individuals managing hypertension (very low sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (zero glycemic load), and cardiovascular disease (zero fat and cholesterol). Its naturally occurring magnesium and calcium contribute trace mineral intake without adding caloric burden. The water has a clean, neutral taste with a slightly smooth mouthfeel attributed to its mineral balance and low total dissolved solids relative to harder mineral waters.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Evian water?
Is Evian water good for diabetics?
Can I drink Evian water if I have high blood pressure?
What minerals are in Evian water?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values for plain mineral water are confirmed by USDA FoodData Central (water, zero macronutrients) and Evian product label data (Danone) for mineral composition. All core macros are zero by nature, which is well-established and consistent across multiple approved sources. GI is confirmed as effectively zero by the University of Sydney GI Database convention for water.
notes: energy_kcal is naturally zero as mineral water contains no caloric macronutrients. protein_g is naturally zero as water contains no protein. fat_g is naturally zero as water contains no fat. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as water contains no carbohydrates or sugars. sugar_g is naturally zero. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. All vitamins are confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for water. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Evian product specification or USDA water entry. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Evian product specification. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the Evian product specification. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the Evian product specification. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are 0.0 as water contains no fat fractions (confirmed zero). glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. Sodium value of 0.65 mg/100 ml derived from Evian label: 6.5 mg/L divided by 10. Calcium 8.0 mg/100 ml from Evian label: 80 mg/L divided by 10. Magnesium 2.6 mg/100 ml from Evian label: 26 mg/L divided by 10. Potassium 0.1 mg/100 ml from Evian label: 1 mg/L divided by 10. The cuisine tag 'Chinese' in the input reflects the restaurant context (Din Tai Fung) rather than the product origin; region_of_origin is correctly set to France. Serving size is 330 ml as stated in the input description.
